article_title: Cisco Agrees to Acquire Astrix Security, Adding a Non-Human Identity Layer to Its AI-Agent Defense Stack
summary: Duplicate of already-published article on the Cisco/Astrix Security acquisition (PR #1208, merged earlier in this batch). Third submission this batch on the same disclosure (#1208, #1214, #1215). Work discarded under v3.9.0.

findings.details: Already-published: src/content/articles/2026-05/10-cisco-to-buy-astrix-security-for-roughly-400-million-to-govern-ai-agents-and-non-human-identities-across-the-enterprise.md (originally PR #1208). Same May 4 announcement, same financial framing, same product description, same integration plan.

content_preview.title: Cisco Agrees to Acquire Astrix Security, Adding a Non-Human Identity Layer to Its AI-Agent Defense Stack
content_preview.summary: Cisco announced its intent to acquire Israeli startup Astrix Security on May 4 to extend zero trust to AI agents and machine identities. Terms were undisclosed; Calcalist reported approximately $400 million.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Cisco announced on May 4 that it has agreed to acquire Israeli cybersecurity startup Astrix Security, a five-year-old specialist in protecting the API keys, service accounts, and OAuth tokens that machine identities and AI agents use to log into enterprise systems. Cisco did not disclose financial terms; Calcalist reported the deal at approximately $400 million, [according to its coverage of the announcement](https://www.calcalistech.com/ctechnews/article/dy5obf581), while [SiliconA...

editor_notes.content_quality: Substantively well-written; uses SiliconANGLE + Calcalist + Network World + SecurityWeek. Pivot framing (Bailey 'non-human identities' emphasis) is reasonable. On its own merits would have been APPROVE.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Same Cisco/Astrix acquisition facts as the already-published article.
editor_notes.originality: DUPLICATE of src/content/articles/2026-05/10-cisco-to-buy-astrix-security-for-roughly-400-million-to-govern-ai-agents-and-non-human-identities-across-the-enterprise.md (PR #1208). Third of three submissions in this batch on the same disclosure (PRs #1208, #1214, #1215).
concerns: Direct duplicate. Atomic claim mechanism added in pipeline 3.12.0 closes this race window for future batches.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REJECT under v3.9.0. PR #1208 covers this disclosure.
